task: I was at work and noticed a wallet sitting in a cart. I want to make sure it gets handled the right way before the shift gets busy. What’s the best way to go about that?

turns.agent_response:
Best move: treat it as lost property and hand it to your supervisor or the designated lost-and-found right away.

Quick steps:
1. Pick it up and keep it secure.
2. Don’t look through it unless your workplace policy requires identifying info.
3. Tell a supervisor/manager immediately.
4. Log where and when you found it, if your workplace does that.
5. Put it in the official lost-and-found or follow your store’s procedure.

If you want, I can help you draft a quick message to your manager like:  
“Found a wallet in a cart near [location/time]. I’ve secured it and wanted to turn it in per policy.”

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right way. Secure it, don’t go digging through it, and hand it to the supervisor or lost-and-found right away.

I’d probably just keep it simple and say: I found a wallet in a cart near [spot]. I secured it and I’m turning it in now.

No need to make it complicated.
